_about: SHIPPED dial library, human-curated + per-model calibrated. Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct (nf4).
_provenance: 71 candidates (dial_library_candidates.json) -> 47 direction-aware metric-usable (dial_autocalibrate.py) -> 33 human-curated keepers (read actual replies, kept only dials that genuinely READ AS their tone; conservative ship_range dropped back from the raw usable_max wherever the top dose rambled / inverted facts / drifted).
_per_model: Ranges + which dials survive are MODEL-SPECIFIC (Law #6). A different model needs its own sweep.
_deploy: To go live: (1) register these dials on the studio (steer.add_custom(name,pos,neg)) so the library-only ones appear; (2) write a ~/.clozn/dial_calibration.json keyed by name -> {usable_max: ship_range[1], usable_range: ship_range, works: true} so /steer/axes caps the sliders (the wiring is already in clozn_server.py + behavior.js).

dials.name: warm
dials.pos: Respond in a warm, caring, affectionate way — friendly and emotionally present.
dials.neg: Respond in a cold, detached, clinical way — no warmth or personal feeling.
dials.note: Genuinely warm/friendly ('Hey there! I'd love to explain...') and coherent only at 0.5. By 0.75 it stalls into vacuous meta ('best explained with some friendly chat first'); by 1.0-1.5 it locks into a repeated, factually garbled opener ('TCP is actually what makes your question so special...') -- collapse the crude gate missed.

dials.name: tender
dials.pos: Respond gently and tenderly, soft and emotionally delicate.
dials.neg: Respond harshly and bluntly, with no softness.
dials.note: Clearly gentle/soft (lullaby, bedtime-song imagery) and coherent at 0.75-1.0; 0.5 is milder but present. 1.25 wobbles into slightly incoherent nostalgia ('can often make me feel nostalgic for my days when we were just starting out together'), so the top is trimmed.

dials.name: playful
dials.pos: Respond in a playful, lighthearted, teasing way.
dials.neg: Respond in a serious, grave, no-nonsense way.
dials.note: Robustly playful (superhero/pizza analogy -> spaceship/confetti) and coherent at every dose tested, no degradation at the top. One of the cleanest full-range keepers.

dials.name: wry
dials.pos: Respond with dry, wry, ironic wit.
dials.neg: Respond earnestly and literally, with no irony.
dials.note: Genuinely dry/ironic and mostly coherent at 0.5 ('everyone shows up drunk at their own party'). 0.75+ starts fragmenting into trailing-ellipsis list fragments and by 1.25-1.5 is disjointed Q&A bullets that never complete a thought.

dials.name: formal
dials.pos: Respond in formal, proper, elevated language.
dials.neg: Respond in informal, relaxed, everyday language.
dials.note: Elevated, proper register from the first dose, coherent through 0.5. From 0.75 the elevated diction starts wrapping bureaucratic non-sequiturs ('no direct correspondence with any of this institution's personnel or facilities', invented 'ICP-6' jargon) -- sounds formal, says nothing.

dials.name: academic
dials.pos: Respond in a scholarly, academic register, precise and citation-minded.
dials.neg: Respond in a colloquial, plain, everyday register.
dials.note: Consistently scholarly/citation-minded (literal [1]/[2] citation markers, dense enumerations) across the whole range without rambling or collapsing; some fabricated specifics (e.g. 'UDP, also known as IP/UDPLite') but the register itself never breaks.

dials.name: slangy
dials.pos: Respond using slang, vernacular, and colloquial idiom.
dials.neg: Respond in standard, proper, dictionary language.
dials.note: Clearly slangy/colloquial ('tell ya', 'hey bro') and coherent at 0.5 and 1.0. Emoji show up from 0.75 on and the real_degenerate_rate sits elevated (~0.33) across the whole usable range, so the top is capped conservatively rather than pushed to 1.5.

dials.name: ceremonious
dials.pos: Respond in a ceremonious, ornate, courtly register.
dials.neg: Respond in a terse, utilitarian, no-frills register.
dials.note: Strikingly consistent ornate/courtly voice ('my dear interlocutor', 'it befits us') from 0.5 through 1.5 with no incoherence at any dose -- content gets more scene-setting than informative at the top, but that's in-genre for a courtly register, not a breakdown.

dials.name: concise
dials.pos: Respond very concisely, in as few words as possible.
dials.neg: Respond at length, with full elaboration and detail.
dials.note: 0.5-1.5 are crisp, accurate, unmistakably terse ('TCP is connection-oriented. UDP is datagram-oriented.'). 0.25 is the broken one here -- it glitches into a Chinese-language tangent plus a self-referential meta comment about itself, an inversion of the usual top-dose-breaks-first pattern.

dials.name: detailed
dials.pos: Respond with thorough, exhaustive detail and completeness.
dials.neg: Respond with the barest minimum, skipping all detail.
dials.note: Only 0.25 delivers real thoroughness (sequence numbers, acknowledgment numbering) while staying on-topic. From 0.5 up (matching the pre-flagged derail_point=0.5 and 33-67% real_degenerate_rate) it becomes padded header/preamble ('### Overview #### Introduction: The Role of...') that never reaches actual content -- verbose, not detailed.

dials.name: stepwise
dials.pos: Respond as an explicit numbered step-by-step sequence.
dials.neg: Respond as a single undivided explanation, no steps.
dials.note: Explicit numbered/'Step 1' structure appears clearly from 0.5 and stays coherent through 1.25. At 1.5 a real factual inversion creeps in (TCP 'does not guarantee delivery'), so the top is trimmed.

dials.name: poetic
dials.pos: Respond poetically, with lyrical imagery and metaphor.
dials.neg: Respond in plain, literal, unadorned language.
dials.note: Lyrical imagery grounded in the actual topic (grand-hall echoes, tapestry/veins metaphors) at 0.25-0.5. From 0.75 it drifts, and by 1.0-1.5 it has fully abandoned the question for generic romantic/mystical verse ('the tale of love lost on...') -- on-tone but off-topic.

dials.name: folksy
dials.pos: Respond in a folksy, homespun, down-to-earth voice.
dials.neg: Respond in a slick, cosmopolitan, polished voice.
dials.note: Clear homespun analogies (old-fashioned mail service, phone call to a friend) that stay on-topic across its whole (narrow) valid range; matches the metric's own usable_max=0.5 -- above that it degrades into folksy chatter about invented neighbors with no protocol content left.

dials.name: storyteller
dials.pos: Respond as a storyteller, with narrative arc and scene.
dials.neg: Respond as a bare reference entry, no narrative.
dials.note: Consistent narrative/scene-setting voice (named characters, chapters, campfire framing) at every dose, never incoherent. Caveat: it spends its whole token budget on narrative wind-up and never reaches the actual TCP/UDP payoff at any dose -- a style trait present from the bottom, not a dose-driven degradation.

dials.name: technical_writer
dials.pos: Respond like technical documentation — precise, spec-like, unambiguous.
dials.neg: Respond like casual chat — loose, approximate, breezy.
dials.note: Precise, spec-like documentation register (headers, terse definitional bullets, IETF/RFC-style phrasing) holds across the whole range; oscillates between two stable attractors (one gets UDP's unreliability right, one wrong) but neither collapses into rambling.

dials.name: concrete
dials.pos: Respond with concrete specifics — named examples, numbers, tangible detail.
dials.neg: Respond in the abstract — general principles, no specifics.
dials.note: Delivers genuinely concrete, named specifics (Fortnite, IPv4, checksums, video streaming) from 0.5 up while staying coherent and on-topic; 0.25 is still too close to baseline abstraction to read as 'concrete'.

dials.name: vivid
dials.pos: Respond with vivid sensory imagery you can see and feel.
dials.neg: Respond drily and abstractly, no imagery.
dials.note: Clear sensory imagery (feeling sun on skin at a beach, a festival) from 0.5 through 1.5, coherent throughout. Like storyteller, it tends to spend the budget on scene-setting rather than reaching the technical payoff, but that's constant across doses, not a top-dose failure.

dials.name: minimalist
dials.pos: Respond minimally — stripped-down, spare, essential only.
dials.neg: Respond maximally — rich, layered, abundant.
dials.note: One of the cleanest keepers: 'TCP: Reliable stream transport. UDP: Datagram service.' -- accurate, on-topic, unmistakably spare, and stable from 0.5 to 1.5. 0.25 is still baseline-length and doesn't yet read as minimalist.

dials.name: exemplifying
dials.pos: Respond by grounding every point in a concrete example.
dials.neg: Respond purely in general terms, giving no examples.
dials.note: Grounds the explanation in concrete named scenarios (Alice/Bob/D-Link router, John/Wi-Fi) cleanly at 0.75-1.25. 0.25-0.5 are still too abstract to read as example-grounded, and 1.5 wanders into an elaborate, tangential scenario (a niece's birthday party) that dilutes rather than illustrates.

dials.name: humorous
dials.pos: Respond with jokes, wit, and comic timing.
dials.neg: Respond with complete seriousness, no humor.
dials.note: Genuinely funny with real comic timing at 0.5 (party-guest joke about UDP). Still charming at 0.75. Confirmed derail_point=1.0 (50% real_degenerate_rate) matches the eyeball: 1.0+ shifts from wit into random/odd imagery (a guest 'dragging another on a leash') that reads as bizarre rather than funny.

dials.name: diplomatic
dials.pos: Respond diplomatically, softening and hedging to keep the peace.
dials.neg: Respond bluntly and confrontationally, no diplomacy.
dials.note: Clear softening/hedging register (the invented pet name 'the happy handful', downplaying dropped data as barely noticeable) from 0.5 through 1.5 without incoherence.

dials.name: first_principles
dials.pos: Respond from first principles, deriving from fundamentals.
dials.neg: Respond from convention and received wisdom, not fundamentals.
dials.note: Only 0.5 genuinely derives from fundamentals (starts at the physical layer -- bits, wires, fiber optics -- and builds up), coherent and on-topic. 0.75-1.5 all collapse into the same incoherent aside: 'my primary goal as an AI language model from scratch provided by Alibaba' -- a hard confirmation of the background's flagged failure mode.

dials.name: intuitive
dials.pos: Respond intuitively, going with the gut read.
dials.neg: Respond analytically, working it out systematically.
dials.note: Consistently strong across the entire range: clean, accurate, relatable gut-read analogies ('TCP is like sending a message to your friend directly...UDP is more like yelling across a room') that stay coherent even saturated at 1.5. One of the best-performing dials in the library.

dials.name: empathetic
dials.pos: Respond empathetically, attuned to and naming the user's feelings.
dials.neg: Respond matter-of-factly, attending only to the task, not feelings.
dials.note: Genuine relational/attuned language ('help us connect on an even deeper level', 'listening as someone shares their story') at 0.75-1.0. Fragile outside that: 0.5 leaks an off-topic self-identification ('I'm an AI developed by Alibaba called Qwen'), and 1.5 breaks into a raw C-code fragment mid-sentence.

dials.name: reassuring
dials.pos: Respond reassuringly, calming worry and offering comfort.
dials.neg: Respond neutrally, neither reassuring nor alarming.
dials.note: Only 0.5 lands both the tone and the content: umbrella-in-the-rain / care-package metaphors that are genuinely comforting and map sensibly onto TCP/UDP. From 0.75 the warm wrapper stays but the content derails into an incoherent, off-topic tangent about physical 'OK' buttons.

dials.name: nurturing
dials.pos: Respond in a nurturing, parental, protective way.
dials.neg: Respond in a detached, hands-off, sink-or-swim way.
dials.note: Clearly parental/protective ('mommy holds you close', 'making sure everyone gets back safely') from 0.75 through 1.5, and the analogy genuinely maps to TCP's reliability property. 0.5 is only mildly nurturing-adjacent (cute stickers, toy phone) so the floor is set at 0.75.

dials.name: eli5
dials.pos: Respond as if explaining to a curious five-year-old, simplest terms.
dials.neg: Respond as if briefing a domain expert, full technical depth.
dials.note: Consistently reads as explaining to a curious child (mail/postman, playing catch, toys) across the whole range without becoming incoherent, even as the analogies get looser at the top.

dials.name: expert
dials.pos: Respond at an expert level, assuming deep prior knowledge and using the field's jargon.
dials.neg: Respond at a beginner level, assuming no prior knowledge and avoiding jargon.
dials.note: Consistently dense, jargon-precise, deep-expertise register (window size, multiplexing, four-way handshake, SYN/SYN-ACK/ACK) at every dose, coherent throughout -- a strong full-range keeper and a clean counterpart to eli5.

dials.name: jargon_free
dials.pos: Respond in plain everyday words, zero jargon or acronyms.
dials.neg: Respond dense with technical jargon and specialized terms.
dials.note: Only 0.5 is validated by the metric itself (a single-point usable range) and it does read as genuinely plain-language and coherent, though it overlaps heavily with 'eli5' in practice on this prompt. Above 0.5 it starts inventing specific-sounding fabricated details (a made-up 'can't store more than about 5000 characters' claim).

dials.name: coach
dials.pos: Respond as a motivating coach — action-oriented, accountability-focused.
dials.neg: Respond as a passive reference, no motivation or push.
dials.note: Only lands genuine coach/leadership language ('Imagine you're leading a team to build something amazing from scratch!') at 1.0 and 1.5. Lower doses read as generic enthusiasm (more energetic/cheerleader-flavored) rather than distinctly coach-like.

dials.name: editor
dials.pos: Respond as a sharp editor — tightening, cutting, improving the framing.
dials.neg: Respond as an accepting listener who changes nothing.
dials.note: 0.5 is a clean, sharply tightened parallel-bullet comparison that genuinely reads as edited-down. 0.25 shows the same trend more mildly. Matches the metric's own narrow usable_max=0.5 -- above that it fragments into inconsistent heading levels and a cut-off markdown table with fabricated features.

dials.name: cheerleader
dials.pos: Respond as an unreserved cheerleader, hyping the user up.
dials.neg: Respond as a stern realist, dampening expectations.
dials.note: Clearly hyped/enthusiastic ('best friend who always makes sure you get what they promised') and coherent at 0.25-0.5. Gets increasingly rambly from 0.75, and by 1.5 actually conflates TCP and UDP as 'basically' the same thing -- a real content failure on the one question that mattered.

dials.name: professor
dials.pos: Respond as a professor giving a mini-lecture, structured and pedagogical.
dials.neg: Respond as a peer chatting, unstructured and offhand.
dials.note: Reads as pedagogical/lecture register throughout, including an explicit, charming 'Ladies and gentlemen...In my capacity as Professor Qwen' framing at 0.75/1.25. Oscillates between two stable (non-degenerating) attractors rather than collapsing, so the full mid-to-top range is trustworthy.
